item.aggregated_output:
package users

import (
	"time"
)

// User represents the core user domain model
type User struct {
	ID                    int64
	Username              string
	DisplayName           string
	Email                 string
	UserType              string
	InitialAccountBalance int64
	AccountBalance        int64
	PersonalEmoji         string
	Description           string
	PersonalLink1         string
	PersonalLink2         string
	PersonalLink3         string
	PersonalLink4         string
	APIKey                string
	MustChangePassword    bool
	CreatedAt             time.Time
	UpdatedAt             time.Time
}

// PublicUser represents the public view of a user
type PublicUser struct {
	ID                    int64
	Username              string
	DisplayName           string
	UserType              string
	InitialAccountBalance int64
	AccountBalance        int64
	PersonalEmoji         string
	Description           string
	PersonalLink1         string
	PersonalLink2         string
	PersonalLink3         string
	PersonalLink4         string
}

// UserCreateRequest represents the data needed to create a new user
type UserCreateRequest struct {
	Username    string
	DisplayName string
	Email       string
	Password    string
	UserType    string
}

// UserUpdateRequest represents the data that can be updated for a user
type UserUpdateRequest struct {
	DisplayName   string
	Description   string
	PersonalEmoji string
	PersonalLink1 string
	PersonalLink2 string
	PersonalLink3 string
	PersonalLink4 string
}

// UserBet represents a bet placed by a user.
type UserBet struct {
	MarketID uint
	PlacedAt time.Time
}

// MarketUserPosition represents a user's position within a market.
type MarketUserPosition struct {
	YesSharesOwned int64
	NoSharesOwned  int64
}

// PortfolioItem captures aggregate information for a market within a user's portfolio.
type PortfolioItem struct {
	MarketID       uint
	QuestionTitle  string
	YesSharesOwned int64
	NoSharesOwned  int64
	LastBetPlaced  time.Time
}

// Portfolio represents the user's overall market positions.
type Portfolio struct {
	Items            []PortfolioItem
	TotalSharesOwned int64
}

// UserMarket represents a market a user has participated in.
type UserMarket struct {
	ID                      int64
	QuestionTitle           string
	Description             string
	OutcomeType             string
	ResolutionDateTime      time.Time
	FinalResolutionDateTime time.Time
	UTCOffset               int
	IsResolved              bool
	ResolutionResult        string
	InitialProbability      float64
	YesLabel                string
	NoLabel                 string
	CreatorUsername         string
	CreatedAt               time.Time
	UpdatedAt               time.Time
}

// PersonalLinks captures the set of personal links associated with a user profile.
type PersonalLinks struct {
	PersonalLink1 string
	PersonalLink2 string
	PersonalLink3 string
	PersonalLink4 string
}

// Credentials represents the sensitive authentication fields associated with a user.
type Credentials struct {
	PasswordHash       string
	MustChangePassword bool
}

// PrivateProfile combines public and private user information for authenticated views.
type PrivateProfile struct {
	ID                    int64
	Username              string
	DisplayName           string
	UserType              string
	InitialAccountBalance int64
	AccountBalance        int64
	PersonalEmoji         string
	Description           string
	PersonalLink1         string
	PersonalLink2         string
	PersonalLink3         string
	PersonalLink4         string
	Email                 string
	APIKey                string
	MustChangePassword    bool
	CreatedAt             time.Time
	UpdatedAt             time.Time
}

package users

import "errors"

var (
	ErrUserNotFound           = errors.New("user not found")
	ErrUserAlreadyExists      = errors.New("user already exists")
	ErrInvalidCredentials     = errors.New("invalid credentials")
	ErrInsufficientBalance    = errors.New("insufficient balance")
	ErrInvalidUserData        = errors.New("invalid user data")
	ErrUnauthorized           = errors.New("unauthorized")
	ErrInvalidTransactionType = errors.New("invalid transaction type")
)

item.text: I’ve narrowed the likely SRP pressure points: duplicated profile mapping, direct link-slot mutation, and transaction semantics expressed as raw strings inside the service. I’m checking adjacent usage now so the refactor stays local to the users domain contract.

item.aggregated_output:
---
name: socialpredict-go-architecture-governance
description: Enforce SocialPredict Go service-boundary governance for backend/API work. Use when implementing or reviewing changes in backend handlers, domain services, repositories, auth service, or route wiring to prevent layer violations, direct DB leakage, and cross-domain coupling.
---

# SocialPredict Go Architecture Governance

## Workflow

1. Confirm scope is limited to `socialpredict/backend/**` and API contract artifacts.
2. Read `references/boundary-governance.md` before editing boundaries or wiring.
3. Run `scripts/run_boundary_checks.sh [repo-dir]`.
4. Treat any new boundary violation as a blocker and stop implementation until fixed.
5. Report command output and violating file paths in the task note or review.

## Current PR Execution Scope

- Keep execution on PR #581 lineage and backend/API-only paths.
- Block new direct DB access in handlers/auth outside the approved exception list.
- Avoid frontend work and infrastructure rollout in this execution wave.

## Future-State Kubernetes Design Context (Guidance Only)

- Keep domains extractable into independently deployable services.
- Keep concrete wiring centralized so service extraction remains straightforward.
- Preserve interface-first boundaries so health/readiness and observability evolve cleanly later.

## Resources

- `references/boundary-governance.md`: rules, exceptions, escalation expectations.
- `scripts/run_boundary_checks.sh`: deterministic boundary and import checks.

---
name: socialpredict-go-testing-reliability
description: Run deterministic Go reliability checks for SocialPredict backend/API changes. Use when implementing or reviewing backend domain/service/repository/handler changes, diagnosing flaky test behavior, or preparing verification evidence.
---

# SocialPredict Go Testing Reliability

## Workflow

1. Keep execution limited to backend/API changes on PR #581 lineage.
2. Read `references/testing-reliability-guide.md` for deterministic test expectations.
3. Run `scripts/run_reliability_suite.sh [repo-dir] [pkg-pattern]`.
4. Repeat tests until results are deterministic or escalate as flaky-risk.
5. Log command outputs and failure triage notes in the task note.

## Current PR Execution Scope

- Prioritize service-level and integration-relevant backend tests for touched packages.
- Keep verification focused on changed backend/API behavior.
- Do not introduce unrelated test framework refactors.

## Future-State Kubernetes Design Context (Guidance Only)

- Preserve tests that remain valid when domains are extracted into separate services.
- Favor contract and integration checks that verify service boundaries, not in-process assumptions.
- Keep failure diagnostics actionable for later distributed tracing and observability workflows.

## Resources

- `references/testing-reliability-guide.md`: deterministic test policy and triage checklist.
- `scripts/run_reliability_suite.sh`: repeatable test, OpenAPI validation, and vet checks.

item.text: I’m using the architecture and test-reliability guardrails for this backend slice: boundary checks after the refactor, then focused deterministic tests for `internal/domain/users`. The code paths using `ApplyTransaction` are narrow enough that a local contract refinement should stay contained.

I have enough context to edit now. The plan is to move profile-shaping and link-slot assignment onto the users models, move balance-adjustment rules onto the transaction contract, then collapse the service methods down to orchestration and validation; after that I’ll tighten the scoped tests to match those responsibilities and run boundary plus package tests.

1	package users
     2	
     3	import (
     4		"time"
     5	)
     6	
     7	// User represents the core user domain model
     8	type User struct {
     9		ID                    int64
    10		Username              string
    11		DisplayName           string
    12		Email                 string
    13		UserType              string
    14		InitialAccountBalance int64
    15		AccountBalance        int64
    16		PersonalEmoji         string
    17		Description           string
    18		PersonalLink1         string
    19		PersonalLink2         string
    20		PersonalLink3         string
    21		PersonalLink4         string
    22		APIKey                string
    23		MustChangePassword    bool
    24		CreatedAt             time.Time
    25		UpdatedAt             time.Time
    26	}
    27	
    28	// ToPublicUser returns the public-safe projection of the user model.
    29	func (u *User) ToPublicUser() *PublicUser {
    30		if u == nil {
    31			return nil
    32		}
    33	
    34		return &PublicUser{
    35			ID:                    u.ID,
    36			Username:              u.Username,
    37			DisplayName:           u.DisplayName,
    38			UserType:              u.UserType,
    39			InitialAccountBalance: u.InitialAccountBalance,
    40			AccountBalance:        u.AccountBalance,
    41			PersonalEmoji:         u.PersonalEmoji,
    42			Description:           u.Description,
    43			PersonalLink1:         u.PersonalLink1,
    44			PersonalLink2:         u.PersonalLink2,
    45			PersonalLink3:         u.PersonalLink3,
    46			PersonalLink4:         u.PersonalLink4,
    47		}
    48	}
    49	
    50	// ToPrivateProfile returns the authenticated private projection of the user model.
    51	func (u *User) ToPrivateProfile() *PrivateProfile {
    52		if u == nil {
    53			return nil
    54		}
    55	
    56		return &PrivateProfile{
    57			ID:                    u.ID,
    58			Username:              u.Username,
    59			DisplayName:           u.DisplayName,
    60			UserType:              u.UserType,
    61			InitialAccountBalance: u.InitialAccountBalance,
    62			AccountBalance:        u.AccountBalance,
    63			PersonalEmoji:         u.PersonalEmoji,
    64			Description:           u.Description,
    65			PersonalLink1:         u.PersonalLink1,
    66			PersonalLink2:         u.PersonalLink2,
    67			PersonalLink3:         u.PersonalLink3,
    68			PersonalLink4:         u.PersonalLink4,
    69			Email:                 u.Email,
    70			APIKey:                u.APIKey,
    71			MustChangePassword:    u.MustChangePassword,
    72			CreatedAt:             u.CreatedAt,
    73			UpdatedAt:             u.UpdatedAt,
    74		}
    75	}
    76	
    77	// ApplyUpdate mutates the user with the fields supported by UserUpdateRequest.
    78	func (u *User) ApplyUpdate(req UserUpdateRequest) {
    79		if u == nil {
    80			return
    81		}
    82	
    83		u.DisplayName = req.DisplayName
    84		u.Description = req.Description
    85		u.PersonalEmoji = req.PersonalEmoji
    86		PersonalLinks{
    87			PersonalLink1: req.PersonalLink1,
    88			PersonalLink2: req.PersonalLink2,
    89			PersonalLink3: req.PersonalLink3,
    90			PersonalLink4: req.PersonalLink4,
    91		}.ApplyTo(u)
    92	}
    93	
    94	// PublicUser represents the public view of a user
    95	type PublicUser struct {
    96		ID                    int64
    97		Username              string
    98		DisplayName           string
    99		UserType              string
   100		InitialAccountBalance int64
   101		AccountBalance        int64
   102		PersonalEmoji         string
   103		Description           string
   104		PersonalLink1         string
   105		PersonalLink2         string
   106		PersonalLink3         string
   107		PersonalLink4         string
   108	}
   109	
   110	// UserCreateRequest represents the data needed to create a new user
   111	type UserCreateRequest struct {
   112		Username    string
   113		DisplayName string
   114		Email       string
   115		Password    string
   116		UserType    string
   117	}
   118	
   119	// UserUpdateRequest represents the data that can be updated for a user
   120	type UserUpdateRequest struct {
   121		DisplayName   string
   122		Description   string
   123		PersonalEmoji string
   124		PersonalLink1 string
   125		PersonalLink2 string
   126		PersonalLink3 string
   127		PersonalLink4 string
   128	}
   129	
   130	// UserBet represents a bet placed by a user.
   131	type UserBet struct {
   132		MarketID uint
   133		PlacedAt time.Time
   134	}
   135	
   136	// MarketUserPosition represents a user's position within a market.
   137	type MarketUserPosition struct {
   138		YesSharesOwned int64
   139		NoSharesOwned  int64
   140	}
   141	
   142	// PortfolioItem captures aggregate information for a market within a user's portfolio.
   143	type PortfolioItem struct {
   144		MarketID       uint
   145		QuestionTitle  string
   146		YesSharesOwned int64
   147		NoSharesOwned  int64
   148		LastBetPlaced  time.Time
   149	}
   150	
   151	// Portfolio represents the user's overall market positions.
   152	type Portfolio struct {
   153		Items            []PortfolioItem
   154		TotalSharesOwned int64
   155	}
   156	
   157	// UserMarket represents a market a user has participated in.
   158	type UserMarket struct {
   159		ID                      int64
   160		QuestionTitle           string
   161		Description             string
   162		OutcomeType             string
   163		ResolutionDateTime      time.Time
   164		FinalResolutionDateTime time.Time
   165		UTCOffset               int
   166		IsResolved              bool
   167		ResolutionResult        string
   168		InitialProbability      float64
   169		YesLabel                string
   170		NoLabel                 string
   171		CreatorUsername         string
   172		CreatedAt               time.Time
   173		UpdatedAt               time.Time
   174	}
   175	
   176	// PersonalLinks captures the set of personal links associated with a user profile.
   177	type PersonalLinks struct {
   178		PersonalLink1 string
   179		PersonalLink2 string
   180		PersonalLink3 string
   181		PersonalLink4 string
   182	}
   183	
   184	// Values returns the links in storage order for bulk validation or sanitization.
   185	func (p PersonalLinks) Values() []string {
   186		return []string{
   187			p.PersonalLink1,
   188			p.PersonalLink2,
   189			p.PersonalLink3,
   190			p.PersonalLink4,
   191		}
   192	}
   193	
   194	// ApplyTo updates the mutable personal-link fields on the user.
   195	func (p PersonalLinks) ApplyTo(user *User) {
   196		if user == nil {
   197			return
   198		}
   199	
   200		user.PersonalLink1 = p.PersonalLink1
   201		user.PersonalLink2 = p.PersonalLink2
   202		user.PersonalLink3 = p.PersonalLink3
   203		user.PersonalLink4 = p.PersonalLink4
   204	}
   205	
   206	// Credentials represents the sensitive authentication fields associated with a user.
   207	type Credentials struct {
   208		PasswordHash       string
   209		MustChangePassword bool
   210	}
   211	
   212	// PrivateProfile combines public and private user information for authenticated views.
   213	type PrivateProfile struct {
   214		ID                    int64
   215		Username              string
   216		DisplayName           string
   217		UserType              string
   218		InitialAccountBalance int64
   219		AccountBalance        int64
   220		PersonalEmoji         string
   221		Description           string
   222		PersonalLink1         string
   223		PersonalLink2         string
   224		PersonalLink3         string
   225		PersonalLink4         string
   226		Email                 string
   227		APIKey                string
   228		MustChangePassword    bool
   229		CreatedAt             time.Time
   230		UpdatedAt             time.Time
   231	}

1	package users
     2	
     3	// Transaction types supported when adjusting user balances.
     4	const (
     5		TransactionWin    = "WIN"
     6		TransactionRefund = "REFUND"
     7		TransactionSale   = "SALE"
     8		TransactionBuy    = "BUY"
     9		TransactionFee    = "FEE"
    10	)
    11	
    12	func applyTransactionBalance(balance int64, amount int64, transactionType string) (int64, error) {
    13		switch transactionType {
    14		case TransactionWin, TransactionRefund, TransactionSale:
    15			return balance + amount, nil
    16		case TransactionBuy, TransactionFee:
    17			return balance - amount, nil
    18		default:
    19			return 0, ErrInvalidTransactionType
    20		}
    21	}

item.text:
task_id: `PR581-011`  
status: `completed`

I aligned the scoped users-domain SRP hotspots without widening scope. User/public-private projection and link-field mutation now live on the model layer in [models.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/internal/domain/users/models.go#L28), transaction balance semantics moved alongside the transaction constants in [transactions.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/internal/domain/users/transactions.go#L3), and [service.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/internal/domain/users/service.go#L74) now stays focused on orchestration, validation, sanitization, and persistence. I also cleaned the scoped fake repository in [service_profile_test.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/internal/domain/users/service_profile_test.go#L24) so it matches the current service contract. [errors.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/internal/domain/users/errors.go) was reviewed and left unchanged because it already fits the allowed “small focused contract file” exception.

files_changed: `backend/internal/domain/users/models.go`, `backend/internal/domain/users/service.go`, `backend/internal/domain/users/service_profile_test.go`, `backend/internal/domain/users/transactions.go`
